content:
✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Well defined, and relatively easy to understand and solve.

Goal
* Please fix the typo: adjectif.

Cases
* Validator 2 contains the word "army". Please modify one of the visible tests so that there is a word ending with "my", such as "dummy", "enemy", "yummy", "astronomy" or "Amy".

* Please add a pair of new cases where each sentence contains one word from every group below:
  • Group 1: mystery, mysteries, myth (or any other word beginning with "my")
  • Group 2: yours, yourself, yourselves
  • Group 3: ours, ourselves
  and the possessive adjective is in all caps (i.e. MY / YOUR / OUR).

* Your uppercase megaword (Test 5): Please revise the sentence for two reasons:
  • There are two possessive adjectives in the sentence (also mentioned by @N3l).
  • The contribution guidelines state that controversial topics should be avoided: https://www.codingame.com/playgrounds/40701/help-center/contribution-guidelines

* Your uppercase megaword (Validator 5): Please change "CIA" to something else for a similar reason.

Thanks.
Hi! You specify "There will always be exactly one possessive adjective from the list provided above in the input sentence" but I count 2 in the POTUS's test :)
Modified (also according to the feedback from @5DN1L), thanks!
I've applied your modifications, let me know if you see anything else :)
Thank you for making the changes. I don't have further comments at the moment 🙂

Ah, one more point:

Constraints & Stub generator
* For the shortest mode, it may be preferable to use 100 instead of 255 (for programming languages that require strings to have a fixed length).

Thanks.
My longest test or validator is ~70 chars, so I've set the limit to 98, so languages like C which also need to include the NUL terminator can use 99 and only use two chars :)
